ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: ERICA Emotional Control Subscale Mean
Notes: Average of items 5 (reversed), 7 (reversed), 8 (reversed), 10 (reversed), 11 (reversed), 13 (reversed), 15 (reversed), and 17 (reversed); 6666= Missing; 9999= Applicable

ElementDescription: ERICA Emotional Self-Awareness Subscale Mean
Notes: Average of items 1, 3, 4, 12 (reversed), and 14 (reversed); 6666= Missing; 9999= Applicable

ElementDescription: ERICA Situational Responsiveness Subscale Mean
Notes: Average of items 2, 6, 9 (reversed), and 16; 6666= Missing; 9999= Applicable

ElementDescription: ERICA Emotional Control Subscale Score

ElementDescription: ERICA Emotional Self-Awareness Subscale Score

ElementDescription: ERICA Situational Responsiveness Subscale Score

ElementDescription: ERICA Sum score (Higher scores indicate greater emotional control)
Notes: Sum of items 1-17 (using reversed scores when indicated); 6666= Missing; 9999= Applicable
